raw_consumer_statement:
I should receive a credit for the charges of {$690.00}. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I ordered ( 3 ) formal dresses to be sent to my home. After 2 weeks my dresses were delivered- on XX/XX/XXXX. 
No dresses were found in my home vicinity or mailbox. When I checked the tracking information, it was delivered by XXXX (? ), no signature, picture, or address confirmation. I have lived at this home for 17 years and never had a delivery issue. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I contacted Customer Service to report my merchandise was not delivered and later received an email that I would not receive a credit or reshipment of the items. I replied to the email XXXX hoping they would reconsider and received no reply. 

On XX/XX/XXXX I again called Customer Service and was told I would receive a credit for my non-delivered purchases. 
On Thursday, XX/XX/XXXX I called regarding the credit not appearing on my account, I was told that information was incorrect and I would not be receiving a credit or a reshipment. I asked to speak with a Supervisor, I was told I would have to wait 10-15 minutes, I asked if the Supervisor could call me and was told the Supervisor would call me on Friday XXXX. Unfortunately, I did not receive a call. 
On Sunday, XX/XX/XXXX I visited the XXXX Macys store and chatted with the On-line Customer Service Representative she told me she could not help but I should call a number she provided and speak with a Supervisor. 

On Monday, XX/XX/XXXX I called the number provided and spoke with a Customer Service Supervisor, she was not able to help me. I finally asked to be transferred to the Credit Card department and initiated a charge dispute. 

Im hoping I can receive a credit for these dresses, this whole experience has been very upsetting and frustrating. I have been a long time Macys shopper and have always received excellent customer service for on-line and in person shopping ( including XXXX at XXXX ) .
